author: @jasonburguess
text: What if you used a cheap socket set to make a universal coupler for the output shaft so it could be interchangeable, just weld on a 3/8" square socket male to female and you could just weld a cheap socket onto whatever you wanted to drive and make a set that would have all the attachments have the same coupler attachment.

author: @StupidLilAdv
text: I might actually exercise if it meant a smaller electric bill.

author: @AndreaDingbatt
text:
Thank you for your enthusiasm, information and motivation!

I have been thinking about how to get as off grid as possible, in the city bedsit I have, so these uploads are certainly a goldmine of ideas, 
   and I will be putting as many into practical use as soon as possible!!

My Hubby just got an electric bill for £600. But he's barely been at home, and hasn't used that amount of electricity ever!!

I hope, that as things come together, we will make some videos, and credits to your good self will be in them all.

As well as asking for your permission 1st, and paying for copyrights etc....
Love your genuine laughter and delight,, by the way!!
Andrea, Jasper and George the Pigeon. XxX.

author: @cragmc8386
text: Thanks for posting all of these interesting videos, have been glued to them! Re the exercise bike generator, have you seen those A frame stands you put your back (conventional bike) wheel on? (Sometimes referred to as turbo trainers) I reckon one of those might be perfect for a project like this. I got one out of Lidl for £40. Every time I'm on it I think I must modify this into a generator somehow. And ultimately charge say a 12v battery. I've watched loads of videos of bike generators on yt etc. and seen some get up into the low hundreds of watts. Ben jandrell has video on yt of a 300w bicycle generator he built. Worth checking out! There's definitely a gap in the market for this sort of thing if you can get the price point right and decent power output

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: the power output is person dependant mate - 400 watts is kind of the maximum - it's what an athlete can produce in a cycle race - normal folks can sustain around 50 - 150 watts per hour depending on fitness level - so pretty much any machine can't produce more than that

replies.author: @cragmc8386
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering did you check out that video mate? He ain't no athlete! No offence Mr Jandrell!

author: @kreynolds1123
text: i understand an athlete can put out about 300 watts. maybe one might selecting a motor/generator capable of producing  50 - 150 watts with peddle power.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: that's the same as I understand it

• a connector to run a dremel

Wishing you well,
Huck

text: I am just wondering if after the output you made, would it be possible to add a second geared flywheel for a much higher RPM so that you could possibly get better generation or even a faster turning output for saw blades, grinding stones, blenders or whatever is required for the add-on needed.

author: @skepticfucker280
text:
Mark 3 needs a big flywheel to store more power.

:)

Awesome upgrade.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: storing more power means you need to put that power in - so though it would do that would be harder to get going - and don't forget this also has work to do! to big a flywheel and you just won't be able to turn it

replies.author: @skepticfucker280
replies.text:
@ThinkingandTinkering just at startup, once spinning takes less work to keep spinning but I agree you don't want the flywheel to big.
Most the free exercise bikes I see for free are the old ones with the big flywheel up front, usually a 20inch steel wheel. 
If the load isn't to big getting it up to speed should be easy, maybe a clutch so it doesn't engage the drive till at a certain rpm. 

Just ideas. 
:)

Cheers and can't wait for the next video.

author: @Meow7081-e3w
text: I would buy this product you’re making I need to lose some weight I don’t know maybe a couple it up to an AC generator to run a few resistive incandescent lightbulbs I know for a fact I can produce 50 W and I’m working for that 50 W. I have physical therapy for my legs and they have a exercise bike I bike on and it has a read out for Watts

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering

replies.author: @thefirstmissinglink
replies.text: I too have had this experience on the exercise bikes at physical therapy and I think everyone needs to keep their expectations within the 50 to 100 watt range. I saw someone suggest running a cement mixer which would be outside of the scope of this particular setup. Cell phones laptops LED lighting would be much better suited for this application.

replies.author: @Meow7081-e3w
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering thanks thats true I certainly would be able to change my phone also speaking of charging I have this project/self made solar phone charger/ power bank I made its the  size of a big bird house. If you want pictures I can send them to you in an email or on Facebook if you have Facebook
